message.content:
You are verifier #2, independent of all other verifiers. Do NOT confirm findings optimistically — only confirm if you can independently verify the issue exists in the actual code at /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay.

Finding F-053: "test_dependabot_monitors_dependencies does not assert GitHub Actions ecosystem is covered"
File: tests/test_supply_chain.py (line 125)
Description: Test verifies only package-ecosystem: pip appears in dependabot.yml. If github-actions block deleted, test passes. Action-pinning drift goes unmonitored.
PoC: Delete github-actions block from dependabot.yml. test_dependabot_monitors_dependencies passes.

Instructions:
1. Read the actual file at /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/tests/test_supply_chain.py
2. Look at the code at line 125 and surrounding context
3. Independently verify whether the described problem EXISTS in the current code
4. Check if the PoC is reproducible from the actual code as written
5. Consider whether this is already fixed (pass-1 #101-#122, pass-2 #124-#156 fixed many issues)
6. Return your independent verdict

Confirmed = true ONLY if: (a) the code problem exists as described, (b) the PoC is reproducible from current code, (c) it is a genuine production-readiness concern. Set confirmed=false for speculative issues, issues already fixed, or issues where the PoC does not match what the code actually does.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# Automated dependency + transitive-update monitoring (#147). Dependabot opens PRs for
2	# vulnerable/outdated dependencies on a weekly cadence so the supply-chain surface is tracked,
3	# not just scanned once. Pairs with the pip-audit gate (reproducible scan of requirements.lock)
4	# and the CycloneDX SBOM artifact in ci.yml.
5	version: 2
6	updates:
7	  # Python packages declared in pyproject.toml (and resolved into requirements.lock).
8	  - package-ecosystem: pip
9	    directory: "/"
10	    schedule:
11	      interval: weekly
12	    open-pull-requests-limit: 10
13	    groups:
14	      # batch low-risk updates so the queue stays reviewable
15	      minor-and-patch:
16	        update-types: ["minor", "patch"]
17	  # The GitHub Actions used by the CI workflows themselves.
18	  - package-ecosystem: github-actions
19	    directory: "/"
20	    schedule:
21	      interval: weekly
22

110	    import tomllib
111	
112	    pp = tomllib.loads((_ROOT / "pyproject.toml").read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
113	    extras = pp["project"]["optional-dependencies"]
114	    offenders = []
115	    for group, deps in extras.items():
116	        for spec in deps:
117	            m = re.search(r">=\s*(\d+)[\d.]*\s*,\s*<\s*(\d+)", spec)
118	            if m:
119	                floor_major, ceil_major = int(m.group(1)), int(m.group(2))
120	                if ceil_major - floor_major > 2:  # allow <floor+2; flag the wide ones
121	                    offenders.append(f"{group}: {spec} spans {ceil_major - floor_major} majors")
122	    assert not offenders, f"over-wide dependency ranges: {offenders}"
123	
124	
125	def test_dependabot_monitors_dependencies():
126	    # #147: automated dependency/transitive update monitoring must exist, not only a one-shot scan.
127	    db = _ROOT / ".github" / "dependabot.yml"
128	    assert db.exists(), "no .github/dependabot.yml (#147)"
129	    text = db.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
130	    assert "package-ecosystem: pip" in text
131	
132	
133	def test_license_gate_present():
134	    # #147: a license gate denies strong-copyleft licenses in the dependency set.
135	    text = _ci_text()
136	    assert "License gate" in text and "pip-licenses" in text and "license_gate.py" in text
137	    gate = (_ROOT / "scripts" / "license_gate.py").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
138	    assert "AGPL" in gate and "GPL" in gate  # denies strong-copyleft families
139	
140	
141	def test_sbom_is_emitted():
142	    # #147: a CycloneDX SBOM artifact is produced for downstream license/transitive monitoring.
143	    text = _ci_text()
144	    assert "cyclonedx-json" in text and "sbom" in text.lower()
145	
146	
147	@pytest.mark.skipif(shutil.which("bash") is None, reason="bash required")
148	def test_allowlist_parse_is_newline_agnostic_and_comment_tolerant(tmp_path):
149	    # #131/#145: the last advisory id must be honored even with no trailing newline, comments and
150	    # blank lines skipped, and ids must not word-split/glob-expand.
151	    f = tmp_path / "ignore"
152	    f.write_bytes(b"# a comment\n\nGHSA-aaaa\nPYSEC-2024-last-no-newline")  # no trailing \n
153	    out = subprocess.run(
154	        ["bash", "-c", _PARSE_LOOP, "_", str(f)],
155	        capture_output=True,
156	        text=True,
157	        check=True,
158	    ).stdout
159	    assert "GHSA-aaaa" in out

content.text: The test at line 125-130 only asserts `"package-ecosystem: pip" in text` — it does not assert anything about `github-actions`. The `dependabot.yml` currently has both blocks, but the test would pass if the `github-actions` block were deleted.

input.reasoning: The test at /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/tests/test_supply_chain.py lines 125-130 only checks that "package-ecosystem: pip" appears in dependabot.yml. It makes no assertion about the github-actions ecosystem block. The dependabot.yml currently contains both a pip block (lines 8-16) and a github-actions block (lines 18-21). If the github-actions block were deleted from dependabot.yml, the test would still pass because the only assertion is `assert "package-ecosystem: pip" in text`. The PoC is directly reproducible from the actual code as written. This is a genuine gap: the test is supposed to verify that automated dependency monitoring exists for all relevant ecosystems (including Actions pinning), but it only enforces the pip ecosystem, leaving github-actions coverage unguarded by the test suite.
